title: Drum Trainer
description: A behavioral learning dataset in which 21 participants acquired two distinct drumming patterns (AABA and AAABAA) across three tempos (60, 100, and 150 bpm) using keyboard responses with real-time visual feedback. The study examines motor sequence learning and timing adaptation through trial-by-trial performance monitoring, with 72 trials per participant across multiple experimental conditions varying pattern complexity, tempo, and hand initiation.
methods_description: Participants performed drumming patterns using f and j keyboard keys with visual feedback (colored circle) indicating response timing (early, on time, or late) relative to target inter-response intervals. Feedback windows were adapted trial-by-trial to maintain approximately 50% on-time responses. Each trial consisted of a fixation period until response followed by a 50 ms feedback circle.
